mechanism_narrative: Declining FEDFUNDS levels predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as cheaper capital stimulates equity valuations and compresses discount rates.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, severe labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds the 9.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive 5-day SPX returns in greater than 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 215.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 215.0 yields positive 20-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence pattern predicts positive 20-day returns in less than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 60-day SPX returns in greater than 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100.0 signals severe supply-side contraction that overrides demand signals, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO below 100.0 coincides with positive 20-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of cases.
